title: Workflow Gate Fixtures
objective: Add fixture-backed regression checks for the bootstrap and gate scripts that a real adopter runs after initialization.
description: Even with an init smoke test, the framework can still regress in `start-slice.sh`, `check-slice-readiness.sh`, `check-pr-readiness.sh`, or `check-scope.sh`. This slice creates reproducible fixtures that cover the happy path and the most important failure paths, especially around `origin`, base branches, and PR-note structure.
must: Create fixture or harness scenarios that exercise `start-slice.sh`, `check-slice-readiness.sh`, `check-pr-readiness.sh`, and `check-scope.sh`
Cover both no-remote and with-remote bootstrap modes for the generated project workflow
Add at least one intentional failure fixture per critical gate so CI proves the error contract and not only the happy path
acceptance: CI fails when a gate script regresses on a covered happy path scenario
CI also fails when an expected failure path stops producing the documented error contract
Maintainers can run the fixture harness locally to reproduce gate failures
assumptions: Fixture repos can live under a repo-local `tests/fixtures/` contract
The gate scripts remain shell-entrypoint tools rather than being rewritten into another runtime
